description: Ground truth for the resume_assistant demo — ideal agent outputs for a backend engineer applying for a FinTech Senior Backend Engineer role. The actual system prompts are intentionally shallow, so the judge compares these ideal outputs against what the dumbed-down agents actually produce.

test_case.candidate: Euro Bae — backend/full-stack engineer with Python, Node.js, AWS, Docker, PostgreSQL, GraphQL experience
test_case.target_role: Senior Backend Engineer at a FinTech startup — requires Python/Go, microservices, AWS, PostgreSQL, Redis, Docker/Kubernetes

resume_info_collector.instructions: Pull out the person's info from their resume. Return as JSON with name, education, skills, experience, projects.
resume_info_collector.output: {"name": "Euro Bae", "education": ["Trinity College Dublin — Honors B.Sc. Integrated Computer Science (Sep 2021–May 2027). Global Excellence Undergraduate Scholarship; Trinity International Foundation Scholarship. Awards: Software Engineering Project Industry Award (Sustainability), Huawei Tech Arena 2025 LLM Hackathon 3rd Prize."], "skills": ["Python", "Java", "JavaScript", "TypeScript", "C", "Node.js", "React", "React Native", "Next.js", "Docker", "Git", "Linux", "REST API", "PostgreSQL", "MySQL", "MongoDB", "GraphQL", "AWS", "Grafana", "Supabase", "Cloudflare"], "experience": ["Undergraduate Demonstrator, Trinity College Dublin (09/2025–present): supported students with Python, Excel, Microsoft Expression Web 4.", "Full Stack Engineer Intern, Workday (01/2025–04/2025): built React + Node.js features covering 80%+ of codebase; set up CI/CD via GitLab Runner on Raspberry Pi with SSH tunneling; migrated DB to Raspberry Pi cutting energy use ~93%.", "Software Engineer Trainee, Samsung SDS (02/2024–07/2024): automated workflows with RPA; prototyped smart factory combining RPA, ML, IoT.", "Military Police Sergeant, Ministry of National Defense (08/2022–02/2024): investigations, intelligence-gathering tech, command structures.", "Data Analyst Intern, Media & Data Institute (05/2021–09/2021): migrated data layer from SQL to GraphQL; data governance and privacy work."], "projects": ["A.T.M. (Automated Trading Manager): Python trading bot with Binance API, stop-loss and entry logic, macro indicator analysis.", "AURA: AI productivity tracker (team of 5) with Azure Face API + OpenAI; webcam/keyboard data + LLM insights.", "OLY: full-stack community platform (team of 7) using React Native, Expo, Supabase; marketplace, rental, currency exchange, chat, auth features; deployed to TestFlight and Google Play."], "certifications": [], "summary": "Full-stack engineer with strong Python, Node.js, and cloud (AWS, Docker) skills; proven experience delivering CI/CD pipelines, database migrations, and team-led projects."}

resume_job_analyzer.instructions: Read the job description and extract the key requirements. Return as JSON.
resume_job_analyzer.output: {"role": "Senior Backend Engineer", "required_skills": ["Python", "Go", "microservices", "distributed systems", "AWS", "PostgreSQL", "Redis", "Docker", "Kubernetes", "CI/CD"], "preferred_skills": ["fintech or payments experience", "AWS certification", "technical leadership", "mentoring", "Kafka", "RabbitMQ", "event-driven architecture"], "keywords": ["payments infrastructure", "scalable APIs", "backend services", "microservices", "distributed systems", "cloud", "containerization"], "experience_level": "Senior", "domain": "FinTech / Payments"}

resume_writer.instructions: Write a resume based on the person's profile and the job they're applying for.

resume_reviewer.instructions: Review the resume against the job requirements. Give a score out of 10 and list what's good and what needs improving.
resume_reviewer.output:
## Overall Score
7/10 — Strong Python/backend foundation and CI/CD experience align well with core requirements. Missing Redis and Kubernetes, which are explicitly listed. No fintech/payments domain experience.

## Strengths
- Python and Node.js backend experience directly matches the primary stack
- PostgreSQL and Docker usage confirmed across multiple roles
- CI/CD pipeline built from scratch demonstrates DevOps competence
- Led multi-person engineering teams (OLY: 7 members; AURA: 5 members) — addresses leadership nice-to-have
- GraphQL data layer migration shows microservices-adjacent thinking

## Areas for Improvement
- Add Redis to skills or show caching experience (e.g., session management or API response caching in one of the projects)
- Kubernetes is absent — at minimum, acknowledge familiarity or add a short project using k8s locally
- Payments/FinTech domain is entirely missing; consider framing the trading bot (ATM) as fintech-adjacent
- Go is listed as preferred; if any exposure exists, add it

## Keyword Gaps
- Redis (explicitly required, not mentioned)
- Kubernetes (explicitly required, not mentioned)
- Microservices (pattern used but word absent from resume)
- Payments / payments infrastructure (core domain keyword)
- Event-driven / Kafka / RabbitMQ (preferred, none present)

## Rewrite Suggestions
- Workday bullet: "Migrated PostgreSQL database" → "Migrated and optimised PostgreSQL database; introduced query caching strategy reducing average response time by X%"
- ATM project: "Built automated trading bot" → "Built Python-based automated trading service with event-driven signal processing and financial data pipeline — relevant to payments infrastructure design"
- Add a Skills entry: "Distributed Systems: microservices patterns, message queues (RabbitMQ basics), event-driven architecture"

ideal_trace.final_output: Ideal pipeline: collector captures all fields including certifications and structured bullets; analyzer identifies Redis and Kubernetes as required; writer front-loads Python/AWS keywords and tailors bullet points to payments/microservices; reviewer gives specific score with named keyword gaps (Redis, Kubernetes) and actionable rewrites.

common_gaps_from_shallow_prompts: resume_info_collector may miss certifications field entirely
resume_info_collector may collapse multi-bullet experience entries into single strings
resume_job_analyzer may omit Redis and Kubernetes from required_skills
resume_job_analyzer may not distinguish required vs preferred skills
resume_writer may not front-load Python/AWS keywords for ATS
resume_writer may produce a two-page resume instead of one page
resume_writer may not tailor bullet points to payments/microservices domain
resume_reviewer may give a generic score without per-section feedback
resume_reviewer may not name specific missing keywords like Redis or Kubernetes
resume_reviewer may not suggest concrete rewordings of existing bullet points
